context: Frontend framework using the App Router paradigm with server components by default, client components for interactive elements (form, chart, auth). Deployed on Vercel's edge network. Chosen for server components, file-based routing, and Vercel deployment.
impact: Enables SSR/SSG for SEO and static content, parallel development with MSW mocks, and direct API calls to FastAPI without a BFF layer.

context: UI library underlying Next.js 15. Used with server and client component patterns. Visx charting library wraps D3 primitives as React components.
impact: Component-based architecture for the entire frontend; enables Visx integration and shadcn/ui primitives.

context: Charting library chosen for the eGFR trajectory chart (Decision #6). Renders to SVG, providing full control over accessibility attributes, custom dash patterns, phase band fills, end-of-line label collision avoidance, and responsive sizing via @visx/responsive ParentSize. Harshit must validate in Sprint 0 POC.
impact: Enables highly customized SVG-based eGFR trajectory visualization with 4 lines, true linear time axis, CKD phase bands, dialysis threshold, and WCAG-compliant accessibility.

context: Lightweight client state management library. Three stores: form-store (lab value form state), ui-store (modals, loading overlays, disclaimer expanded state), and auth-store (session token, user object, isAuthenticated flag). JWT access tokens stored in Zustand (in-memory).
impact: Manages UI state, auth tokens, and form values without the complexity of Redux. Keeps JWT access tokens in memory for security.

context: Server state management library for API caching, mutations, and optimistic updates. Handles predict, lab-entries, and auth endpoint mutations. Every chart render requires a fresh POST /predict (compute-on-demand).
impact: Manages API communication, caching, and server state synchronization. Enables clean separation of server state from client state (Zustand).

context: Utility-first CSS framework for mobile-first responsive design. Design tokens from Inga map directly to Tailwind CSS variables. Breakpoints: sm (640px), md (768px), lg (1024px), xl (1280px). Used with shadcn/ui components.
impact: Enables rapid, consistent styling with design token integration, responsive layouts, and accessibility-compliant spacing/sizing for the 60+ patient audience.

context: Accessible, composable component library built on Radix UI primitives. Copy-paste model avoids dependency bloat. Provides baseline ARIA roles and keyboard navigation. Used for Button, Input, RadioGroup, Card, Select, and other form/layout primitives.
impact: Provides accessible-by-default UI components that map to Tailwind design tokens. Eliminates need for custom accessibility work on standard form elements.

context: API mocking tool for parallel frontend development. Intercepts API calls during development and testing, returning responses matching the published OpenAPI 3.1 contract. Mock handlers cover POST /lab-entries, POST /predict, auth endpoints, and error responses. Mock data accuracy improves once Lee's test vectors arrive.
impact: Enables frontend development to proceed in parallel with backend development using contract-accurate mock responses. Contract tests validate MSW mocks stay aligned with the real API.

context: Backend framework serving all endpoints under /api/v1/. Chosen for native async support, automatic OpenAPI generation from Pydantic models, and strong validation. The proprietary rules engine runs as an embedded Python module within FastAPI. Containerized with Docker and deployed to AWS ECS/Fargate.
impact: Provides the REST API layer with built-in validation, OpenAPI spec generation, and async performance. Hosts the proprietary prediction engine as an embedded module.

context: Data validation library used with FastAPI for request/response model validation. Pydantic models enforce all field types, ranges (BUN 5-150, creatinine 0.3-15.0, etc.), and required/optional field rules. The API contract is auto-generated from Pydantic models and validated against the handwritten OpenAPI spec.
impact: Provides first-line defense for input validation; models serve as both documentation and runtime validation. CHECK constraints in PostgreSQL provide defense-in-depth.

context: Primary database on AWS RDS with KMS encryption. Five MVP tables: users, lab_entries, guest_sessions, magic_link_tokens, audit_log. Uses ACID transactions, JSONB for flexible guest session storage, CHECK constraints for defense-in-depth validation, UUID PKs via gen_random_uuid(), TIMESTAMPTZ for all timestamps, INET for IP addresses, and NUMERIC for exact decimal lab values.
impact: Provides HIPAA-compliant persistent storage with strong constraint support, native UUID generation, and mature encryption via AWS RDS/KMS.

context: PostgreSQL extension providing gen_random_uuid() for native UUID v4 generation on all application table primary keys, and cryptographic functions for SHA-256 token hashing. Eliminates need for application-side UUID libraries.
impact: Enables native UUID generation and cryptographic hashing at the database layer, supporting passwordless magic link token storage.

context: PostgreSQL extension for in-database job scheduling. Used for the guest session purge cron job that runs every 15 minutes: DELETE FROM guest_sessions WHERE expires_at < NOW(). Recommended for MVP simplicity over application-level alternatives (APScheduler, Celery beat). Requires the extension to be enabled on RDS (available on PostgreSQL 12.5+).
impact: Ensures HIPAA-compliant automated purge of expired guest session PHI data without application-level infrastructure dependencies.

context: Python ORM used with Alembic for database migrations and model definitions. SQLAlchemy models mirror the PostgreSQL schema and inform Pydantic request/response models in FastAPI.
impact: Provides the ORM layer between FastAPI and PostgreSQL; powers Alembic migration autogeneration from model changes.

context: Database migration tool for versioned, repeatable schema migrations. Migrations run under the app_admin role (DDL privileges). All environments (local dev, CI, staging, production) use the same migration chain. Initial migration creates all tables, indexes, constraints, triggers, and grants from db_schema.sql.
impact: Ensures consistent database schema across all environments with versioned, auditable migrations. Supports rollback via downgrade() functions.

context: Frontend unit and component testing framework. Native ESM support, fast HMR, aligns with Next.js 15 App Router. Used with React Testing Library for component tests. Target: 85% line coverage (PR-blocking). Coverage via c8/Istanbul.
impact: Provides fast, modern frontend unit testing with 85% coverage threshold enforced in CI.

context: Component testing library that enforces accessible queries (getByRole, getByLabelText). Used with Vitest for unit and component tests. Ensures tests assert on user-visible behavior rather than implementation details.
impact: Enforces accessibility-aware testing patterns; ensures frontend components are testable from a user's perspective.

context: Backend unit and integration testing framework for FastAPI/Python. Used with pytest-asyncio for async support and httpx AsyncClient for full request lifecycle testing without running the server. Target: 90% line coverage (PR-blocking). Coverage via coverage.py.
impact: Provides comprehensive backend testing with 90% coverage threshold enforced in CI. httpx AsyncClient enables FastAPI-recommended async integration testing.

context: E2E testing framework for cross-browser testing (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit), mobile viewport emulation, and built-in accessibility scanning via @axe-core/playwright. Covers 8 active critical user journeys plus visual regression testing for Visx SVG chart output. Screenshot comparison validates chart rendering stability.
impact: Enables cross-browser E2E testing, mobile responsive validation, automated WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility scanning, and visual regression baselines for the eGFR trajectory chart.

context: API contract testing tool that auto-generates tests from the OpenAPI 3.1 spec. Validates every endpoint/method/status combination, boundary values, missing fields, and malformed requests. Preferred over Dredd for OpenAPI 3.1 support. Runs against /artifacts/api_contract.json.
impact: Ensures 100% contract endpoint coverage (PR-blocking). Validates that both frontend mocks (MSW) and real API implementation conform to the published OpenAPI spec.

context: Automated WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility scanning tool integrated into Playwright E2E tests. Scans run after each E2E test step. Catches ~60% of WCAG violations; remaining 40% requires manual screen reader testing (VoiceOver). Release-blocking: 0 critical/serious violations allowed.
impact: Provides automated accessibility enforcement for the 60+ CKD patient audience. Ensures all UI components meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast, touch target, and semantic requirements.

context: Performance and load testing tool chosen over Locust for scriptability and CI integration. Validates prediction API response time under concurrent load. Target: POST /predict <2s p95 at 100 concurrent users. Runs nightly only (not on every PR).
impact: Ensures the prediction API meets performance targets under realistic load, supporting the soft launch (100 patients) and scaling to 600 concurrent users.

context: Containerization for the FastAPI backend (deployed to ECS/Fargate) and local development environment. Docker Compose runs FastAPI + PostgreSQL + MailHog for local dev. All environments (dev, staging, production) use the same containerized backend.
impact: Provides consistent development and deployment environments. Local dev mirrors production topology with MailHog for magic link email testing.

context: Deployment platform for the Next.js 15 frontend. Serves static assets and server-rendered pages from the edge network. Part of the three-tier deployment: Next.js on Vercel (edge), FastAPI on AWS ECS/Fargate, PostgreSQL on AWS RDS.
impact: Provides edge deployment, automatic SSL, and optimized Next.js hosting for the frontend application.

context: CI/CD pipeline for the full test and deployment workflow: Lint -> Unit Tests -> Integration Tests -> Contract Tests -> E2E Tests -> Accessibility Scan -> Coverage Report -> Deploy to Staging (auto on merge to main) -> Deploy to Production (manual gate). PRs that fail any stage are blocked from merging. Coverage reports posted as PR comments. Nightly: full regression + visual regression + load tests.
impact: Enforces the full quality pipeline on every PR, automates staging deployments, and provides manual production deployment gates.

context: API specification format used as the single source of truth between frontend and backend. The handwritten spec is authoritative; FastAPI's auto-generated spec is validated against it. Published to /artifacts/api_contract.json. Defines all 12 MVP endpoints, request/response schemas, error envelope, security schemes, CORS policy, and example responses for mock data.
impact: Enables contract-first parallel development: frontend builds against mocks, backend builds against the spec, and Schemathesis validates both against the same contract.

context: SMTP testing tool used in the local Docker Compose development environment for magic link email testing. Captures outbound emails sent by FastAPI during magic link authentication flow without actually delivering them.
impact: Enables local testing of the magic link auth flow without an external email service. Developers can inspect magic link emails in the MailHog UI.

context: Managed PostgreSQL hosting on AWS with AES-256 encryption at rest via KMS customer-managed keys. Encryption covers data files, automated backups, read replicas, and snapshots. Key rotation annually per HIPAA Security Rule. TLS 1.2+ enforced on all connections (rds.force_ssl=1). 35-day automated backup retention with point-in-time recovery.
impact: Provides HIPAA-compliant encrypted database hosting with automated backups and enforced encryption in transit.

context: Serverless container orchestration for the FastAPI backend. Provides auto-scaling and zero-server management. Part of the three-tier deployment alongside Vercel (frontend) and RDS (database).
impact: Enables auto-scaling backend deployment without managing server infrastructure. Supports the scale from 100 patients (soft launch) to 600 concurrent users.

context: Code coverage tools: coverage.py for Python/FastAPI backend (--fail-under=90), Istanbul/c8 for frontend via Vitest (85% threshold). Coverage reports generated per PR and posted as PR comments. Generated code and third-party library internals are excluded.
impact: Enforces coverage thresholds in CI. Backend: 90% line coverage (PR-blocking). Frontend: 85% line coverage (PR-blocking).

context: Typography choice specified by Inga (UX/UI Designer). Selected for excellent number rendering, critical for lab values and chart labels in the eGFR trajectory visualization. Typography scale ranges from 12px caption to 28px display.
impact: Ensures clear, readable numeric display for clinical lab values across all screen sizes, particularly important for the 60+ patient audience.
